issue.title: Harden clean-space credential storage in scrubs guests

user-story.as-a: scrubs VM user with guest-local clean credentials
user-story.i-want: clean-space tokens such as GitHub CLI and Codex credentials to avoid plaintext-at-rest storage by default
user-story.so-that: the remaining clean-side blast radius is reduced even if dirty execution controls improve only incrementally

issue.premise:
The current scrubs model intentionally separates clean and dirty execution, but some clean-space credentials are still only as safe as their at-rest storage. Today, `gh auth login` in a scrubs guest reports that authentication credentials were saved in plain text, which is consistent with GitHub CLI behavior when no usable system credential store is available. The guest currently does not provision a secret-service style keyring or another clearly supported secure backend for `gh`, and the same broader question applies to other clean-space credentials such as Codex authentication.
This means the bubblewrap boundary helps protect clean credentials from dirty execution, but the clean credentials themselves may still be stored more weakly than we want. Hardening this area should focus on how clean credentials persist at rest, whether plaintext fallback should be forbidden by policy, and whether scrubs should prefer ephemeral environment injection, a guest-local credential manager, or another explicit storage model.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Scrubs has an explicit policy for clean-space credential persistence, including whether plaintext storage is ever permitted.

`gh auth login` in a scrubs guest no longer silently falls back to plaintext storage, either because a supported secure backend is available or because scrubs fails closed when secure storage is unavailable.
The chosen storage model is documented for both GitHub CLI and Codex authentication, including where credentials live and how they are revoked or rotated.
Dirty execution remains unable to read or invoke the resulting clean credential stores under the bubblewrap boundary.
At least one validation path demonstrates the final behavior end to end, including the expected `gh auth status` or equivalent evidence of where credentials are stored.

review-items.item: Decide whether scrubs should provision a guest-local credential store, forbid persistent CLI login and rely on ephemeral environment variables, or support multiple approved modes with a clear default.
Identify how Codex authentication is currently stored in guests and whether its persistence model should match GitHub CLI or be handled differently.

Decide whether clean-space git authentication should continue to rely on GitHub CLI integration, a git credential helper, SSH keys, or a separate scrubs-specific approach once credential storage is hardened.
